extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE won with a late surge inside 3km after ROGLIČ attacked earlier, positioning himself perfectly in the decisive final group.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ attacked inside 3km forcing selection before finale, revealing Jumbo-Visma willing to test rivals before mountain stages.
ALAPHILIPPE repeatedly looked over shoulder to assess who remained, using intelligence gathering before committing his final acceleration.
Bora-Hansgrohe isolated with only FORMOLO in final group, showing vulnerability when Quick-Step controls positioning through narrow roads.
CLARKE bridged with ROGLIČ group demonstrating EF positioning value, but lacked finishing speed against WorldTour sprinters.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: first individual stage win at Tirreno after losing time day prior, showing resilience and tactical awareness under pressure
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: dangerous enough inside 3km that peloton reacted immediately, establishes GC threat status even on semi-sprint finishes
rider_signals.CLARKE Simon: neutral: made decisive move with ROGLIČ but lacks pure speed, confirms breakaway rather than finale specialist
rider_signals.FORMOLO Davide: negative: only Bora rider in final group reveals team's inability to position multiple cards on technical finishes
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: retained race lead but ALAPHILIPPE closed 27 seconds, showing white jersey vulnerability if Quick-Step applies pressure
